title: JDBC Connection Lifecycle Flow
interaction_flow: Caller → SqlServerConnection: new SqlServerConnection(SqlServerConnectorConfig, SqlServerValueConverters, skippedOperations, useSingleDatabase)
JdbcConnection: stores config, factory, initialOps, queryTimeout=config.getQueryTimeout().toSeconds(), conn=null
SqlServerConnection → buildGetAllChangesForTableQuery(dataQueryMode, skippedOperations): builds CDC query string
JdbcConnection → JdbcConnection: isConnected() → conn==null? return false
ConnectionFactoryDecorator → ConnectionFactory: connect(config) [checks for custom factory class]
ConnectionFactory → DriverManager: getConnection(url, props) [url built from pattern with host/port/db substitution]
ConnectionFactoryDecorator → JdbcConnection: return Connection (conn field set)
JdbcConnection → JdbcConnection: createStatement() → connection().createStatement() + initializeStatement(stmt, queryTimeout)
JdbcConnection → PreparedStatement: initializeStatement(stmt) [sets queryTimeout via setQueryTimeout]
JdbcConnection: returns cached PreparedStatement (LIRS eviction cache, capacity=10,000)
JdbcConnection → JdbcConnection: createPreparedStatement(sql) [cache hit or miss]
Caller → SqlServerConnection: getChangesForTable(changeTable, fromLsn, seqval, opFrom, toLsn, maxRows)
SqlServerConnection: builds SQL query from getAllChangesForTable template (FUNCTION or DIRECT mode)
SqlServerConnection → JdbcConnection: connection() → returns existing Connection
SqlServerConnection → PreparedStatement: setBytes/setInt for LSN range parameters
SqlServerConnection → Caller: return ResultSet (raw, not wrapped)
Caller → CancellableResultSet: from(resultSet) [static factory, wraps ResultSet]
Caller → CancellableResultSet: next() → delegates to resultSet.next()
Caller → CancellableResultSet: getXxx(col) → delegates to resultSet.getXxx(col)
CancellableResultSet: if !isClosed() && !isAfterLast() → getStatement().cancel() [server-side cancel, saves cycling rows]
Caller → JdbcConnection: execute(String... sqls) → createStatement() → ops.apply(stmt) → commit()
Caller → JdbcConnection: rollback() → connection().rollback() [synchronized, only if !autoCommit]
Caller → JdbcConnection: executeWithoutCommitting(sqls) → createStatement().execute() [throws if autoCommit=true]
JdbcConnection → CallPreparer: accept(callableStatement) [bind params if not null]
alt [close succeeds] → Connection: close() → 'Connection gracefully closed'
key_relationships: CancellableResultSet implements java.sql.ResultSet (Decorator pattern over ResultSet)
JdbcConnection composes statementCache: BoundedConcurrentHashMap<String, PreparedStatement> (LIRS, cap=10000)
ConnectionFactoryDecorator wraps default ConnectionFactory + optional custom factory (via config class name)
JdbcConnection uses RelationalDatabaseConnectorConfig indirectly via JdbcConfiguration for queryTimeout and queryFetchSize

key_methods: patternBasedFactory(String, String driverClass, ClassLoader, Field...): ConnectionFactory [static]
close(): void — cancels parent Statement if ResultSet open and not exhausted, then closes delegate
getXxx(col): value — all delegate to resultSet
CancellableResultSet.purpose: Server-side statement cancel on early close — avoids iterating all rows for large snapshot queries

key_methods: connection(boolean): Connection [override, sets autoCommit=false on new connection]
getChangesForTable(SqlServerChangeTable, Lsn, Lsn, Lsn, int maxRows): ResultSet

important_notes: JdbcConnection is @NotThreadSafe; synchronized methods are connection() and close() only
SqlServerConnection always sets autoCommit=false on new connections — all writes require explicit commit()
PreparedStatement cache uses LIRS eviction (capacity 10,000); evicted statements are auto-closed
CancellableResultSet.close() calls Statement.cancel() server-side ONLY when ResultSet is open and not after last row — optimizes large snapshot fetches
Connection close uses 10-second timeout; on timeout falls back to conn.abort() (force close)
ON_CONNECT_STATEMENTS config key allows arbitrary SQL run on each new connection
initialOps is run on every new connection before ON_CONNECT_STATEMENTS
SqlServerConnection.getChangesForTable() uses DataQueryMode (FUNCTION vs DIRECT) — FUNCTION mode calls fn_cdc_get_all_changes_*, DIRECT mode reads CDC table directly
queryTimeout from RelationalDatabaseConnectorConfig is applied to all statements via initializeStatement()
queryFetchSize from config controls PreparedStatement.setFetchSize() for CDC queries
